title: Prompt Caching and Semantic Caching Economics

questions.question: What is the difference between L1 semantic caching and L2 prompt/prefix caching?
options: L1 skips the LLM entirely on embedding similarity hits; L2 reuses attention KV at the provider for repeated prefixes
L1 is provider-side and L2 is client-side
L2 stores embeddings; L1 stores attention KV
L1 and L2 are the same
questions.question_zh: L1 语义缓存与 L2 prompt/prefix 缓存有什么区别？
options_zh: L1 在 embedding 相似度命中时直接跳过 LLM；L2 在供应商侧复用重复前缀的 attention KV
L1 在供应商侧，L2 在客户端
L2 存 embedding，L1 存 attention KV
L1 和 L2 是同一回事

questions.question: Which Anthropic mechanism marks blocks as cacheable for L2 prompt caching?
options: Explicit cache_control attribute on the request blocks
A separate /caches endpoint
Filename suffixes in tool definitions
An implicit prompt-length threshold
questions.question_zh: Anthropic 用什么机制把 block 标记为 L2 prompt cache 可缓存？
options_zh: 请求 block 上显式的 cache_control 属性
一个独立的 /caches 端点
工具定义里的文件名后缀
一个隐式的 prompt 长度阈值

questions.question: How does the parallelization anti-pattern inflate the bill?
options: Parallel requests bypass batching
Parallelization triggers a per-request guardrail charge
All parallel requests share one cache entry automatically
N parallel requests with the same prefix arrive before the first cache write completes, so each pays a write premium and gets zero discount
questions.question_zh: parallelization 反模式是怎么把账单变贵的？
options_zh: 并行请求绕开了 batching
并行会触发每请求的 guardrail 计费
所有并行请求会自动共享一个缓存条目
N 个使用相同前缀的并行请求在第一次缓存写入完成前就都到了，每个都要付写入溢价并且都拿不到折扣

questions.question: What is the dynamic-content anti-pattern in cacheable prefixes?
options: Putting tool schemas in the prefix
Always streaming responses
Including content that changes every request (current time to the minute, request ID, randomized example order) inside the cacheable prefix, killing hit rate
Using too short a system prompt
questions.question_zh: 可缓存前缀里的 dynamic-content 反模式是什么？
options_zh: 把 tool schema 放进前缀
总是用流式响应
把每次请求都会变的内容（精确到分钟的当前时间、请求 ID、随机示例顺序）放进可缓存前缀，把命中率打没
system prompt 太短

questions.question: How can batch + cached input stack overnight to cut cost?
options: Batch only saves output cost
Batch APIs give 50% off; cached input adds another ~10x; combined, overnight pipelines drop to ~10% of synchronous-uncached cost
Batch is incompatible with caching
Caching disables batch eligibility
questions.question_zh: batch + 缓存输入怎么在过夜任务里叠出大幅降本？
options_zh: Batch 只省 output 成本
Batch API 给 50% 折扣；缓存输入再叠约 10x；合在一起，过夜 pipeline 大约能降到同步未缓存成本的 ~10%
Batch 与缓存不兼容
缓存会让 batch 失去资格

questions.question: What does the lesson say about semantic cache "95% accuracy" claims?
options: 95% is the OpenAI default cache hit rate
95% refers to match correctness, not hit rate; reported production hit rates range from ~10% (open chat) up to ~70% (structured FAQ)
95% is a vendor-documented hit-rate baseline
95% means you should expect 95% cache hits
questions.question_zh: 本课怎么看 “95% accuracy” 的语义缓存声明？
options_zh: 95% 是 OpenAI 默认的命中率
95% 指的是匹配正确率，不是命中率；公开的生产命中率范围从 ~10%（开放聊天）到 ~70%（结构化 FAQ）
95% 是供应商文档中的命中率基线
95% 意味着你应当期望 95% 的缓存命中
